name: sends_empty_despite_nonempty_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_sends_empty_despite_nonempty_input(net_events: Seq<NetEvent>, res: EventResults)
    requires
        net_events.len() > 0,
        forall |i: int| 0 <= i && i < net_events.len() ==> net_events[i] is Send,
        res.recvs == Seq::<NetEvent>::empty(),
        res.clocks == Seq::<NetEvent>::empty(),
        res.sends == net_events,
        res.ios == net_events,
        res.event_seq() == net_events,
        res.well_typed_events(),
    ensures
        res.sends.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: A non-empty all-Send input producing empty sends would mean the spec loses track of outgoing events

name: empty_input_nonempty_event_seq
code:
proof fn phi_2_empty_input_nonempty_event_seq(net_events: Seq<NetEvent>, res: EventResults)
    requires
        net_events.len() == 0,
        forall |i: int| 0 <= i && i < net_events.len() ==> net_events[i] is Send,
        res.recvs == Seq::<NetEvent>::empty(),
        res.clocks == Seq::<NetEvent>::empty(),
        res.sends == net_events,
        res.ios == net_events,
        res.event_seq() == net_events,
        res.well_typed_events(),
    ensures
        res.event_seq().len() > 0,
{
}
reason: An empty input producing a non-empty event sequence would fabricate events out of nothing

name: sends_contain_receive_event
code:
proof fn phi_3_sends_contain_receive_event(net_events: Seq<NetEvent>, res: EventResults)
    requires
        net_events.len() > 0,
        forall |i: int| 0 <= i && i < net_events.len() ==> net_events[i] is Send,
        res.recvs == Seq::<NetEvent>::empty(),
        res.clocks == Seq::<NetEvent>::empty(),
        res.sends == net_events,
        res.ios == net_events,
        res.event_seq() == net_events,
        res.well_typed_events(),
    ensures
        exists |i: int| 0 <= i && i < res.sends.len() && res.sends[i] is Receive,
{
}
reason: Having a Receive inside sends would violate well_typed_events and corrupt the event classification invariant

name: event_seq_longer_than_sends
code:
proof fn phi_4_event_seq_longer_than_sends(net_events: Seq<NetEvent>, res: EventResults)
    requires
        forall |i: int| 0 <= i && i < net_events.len() ==> net_events[i] is Send,
        res.recvs == Seq::<NetEvent>::empty(),
        res.clocks == Seq::<NetEvent>::empty(),
        res.sends == net_events,
        res.ios == net_events,
        res.event_seq() == net_events,
        res.well_typed_events(),
    ensures
        res.event_seq().len() > res.sends.len(),
{
}
reason: event_seq being longer than sends (with empty recvs/clocks) would mean concatenation conjured phantom events

name: ios_differs_from_event_seq
code:
proof fn phi_5_ios_differs_from_event_seq(net_events: Seq<NetEvent>, res: EventResults)
    requires
        net_events.len() > 0,
        forall |i: int| 0 <= i && i < net_events.len() ==> net_events[i] is Send,
        res.recvs == Seq::<NetEvent>::empty(),
        res.clocks == Seq::<NetEvent>::empty(),
        res.sends == net_events,
        res.ios == net_events,
        res.event_seq() == net_events,
        res.well_typed_events(),
    ensures
        res.ios != res.event_seq(),
{
}
reason: ios diverging from event_seq would break the refinement contract between intended and observed events
